touches.ai_summary: Inbound request from Wyatt Yang (Director of CS) expressing interest in improving pipeline visibility for LunarVault's sales team. Wyatt mentioned challenges with current reporting across 51 sales team members and asked about automation capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Does your platform integrate with our existing CRM stack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs and can typically complete setup within a week.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call to understand current pain points

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent to Wyatt confirming receipt of inbound request and proposing three time slots for initial qualification call. Included brief overview of pipeline visibility features and case study from similar managed IT company.
next_steps: Awaiting Wyatt's availability confirmation

touches.ai_summary: Wyatt confirmed Thursday 2pm slot for qualification call. Brief exchange where Wyatt mentioned he recently joined LunarVault (3 months ago) and is building out their CS operations infrastructure. Expressed urgency around Q2 planning cycle.

questions_asked.question: Will we need to involve IT for implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal IT involvement - mostly configuration rather than technical integration. We can discuss technical requirements on our call.

next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for Thursday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: 12-minute qualification call with Wyatt. Discussed LunarVault's current challenge: sales managers lack real-time pipeline visibility, leading to forecast inaccuracy. Team uses manual spreadsheets to supplement CRM. Wyatt identified need for automated reporting and forecasting for 51-seat sales team across enterprise accounts.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can your team get us up and running?
questions_asked.answer: Typical implementation is 2-3 weeks for a team your size, including training and custom dashboard setup.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the timeline around Q2?
questions_asked.answer: New leadership wants better visibility before mid-year planning. I need to show progress on operations improvements.

next_steps: Wyatt to share current reporting examples
Schedule discovery call with broader context

touches.ai_summary: 15-minute follow-up call to review Wyatt's current reporting examples. Identified specific gaps: deal stage progression tracking, rep activity metrics, and executive dashboards. Discussed involving Riley White (CRO) and sales management team in discovery. Wyatt enthusiastic about automation + consulting package.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Definitely Riley White, our CRO - he's been pushing for better pipeline tools. Also our sales managers Leo Mitchell and Avery Wang who'd be primary users.

questions_asked.question: What does the consulting component cover?
questions_asked.answer: We provide best-practice implementation guidance, custom workflow design, and ongoing optimization based on your evolving needs.

next_steps: Wyatt to coordinate Riley's calendar
Prepare discovery deck focused on enterprise pipeline visibility

touches.ai_summary: Email to Wyatt with discovery call preparation materials: agenda, stakeholder questions, and request for current tech stack documentation. Wyatt responded confirming discovery call scheduled for next Tuesday with extended time blocked for thorough discussion.
next_steps: Discovery call Tuesday 10am
Wyatt sending tech stack details by Friday

touches.ai_summary: 25-minute discovery call with Wyatt diving deep into LunarVault's pipeline management workflows. Current pain: sales managers spend 8+ hours weekly compiling reports manually. Enterprise deal cycles (6-9 months) require granular stage tracking. Wyatt explained his mandate to modernize CS/RevOps infrastructure after joining 3 months ago. Discussed 51-seat deployment across sales org.

questions_asked.question: What's worked well with your current process that we should preserve?
questions_asked.answer: Our sales managers are very detail-oriented - they need drill-down capability, not just high-level dashboards.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle enterprise accounts differently than SMB in your system?
questions_asked.answer: We offer custom field configurations and workflow rules - you can set up distinct pipelines for different segments.

questions_asked.question: What's Riley's biggest concern about pipeline visibility right now?
questions_asked.answer: Forecast accuracy. We've missed quarterly targets twice because we couldn't see slippage early enough.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with Avery Wang
Prepare ROI analysis based on 8hrs/week time savings

touches.ai_summary: 39-minute technical evaluation with Avery Wang (Sales Manager). Walked through platform capabilities: custom dashboards, automated reporting, forecasting models, and CRM integration architecture. Avery focused on data accuracy and real-time sync requirements. Discussed API capabilities and webhook configurations for their existing tech stack.

questions_asked.question: How does your forecasting model handle enterprise deal cycles that are 6-9 months long?
questions_asked.answer: Our ML models adjust for cycle length by segment - you can set custom probability curves based on historical data for enterprise vs other segments.

questions_asked.question: What's the data refresh rate for real-time dashboards?
questions_asked.answer: Live sync with 30-second latency for most CRM platforms. Critical updates like stage changes trigger immediate webhooks.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a must-have vs nice-to-have for your team?
questions_asked.answer: Saving our managers those 8 hours a week would be huge. But we need to make sure data integrity is bulletproof - can't have discrepancies between systems.

objections: Need to verify our CRM API rate limits can handle real-time sync - I'll need to check with our platform admin
next_steps: Avery to validate technical requirements with IT
Schedule follow-up technical session

touches.ai_summary: 35-minute follow-up technical evaluation with Avery Wang (rescheduled from previous week). Reviewed API rate limit analysis - confirmed compatibility. Avery walked through specific reporting requirements for sales managers Leo, Blake, and himself. Discussed user permissions model and data security. Avery more engaged after resolving technical concerns.

questions_asked.question: How granular can we get with role-based permissions?
questions_asked.answer: Field-level permissions by role, team, or individual. Managers can see their team's full pipeline while reps see only their own deals.

questions_asked.question: Are you comfortable recommending this to Riley and the procurement team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, assuming pricing works. Technically it meets our needs. I'd want to see a phased rollout plan though.

questions_asked.question: What does phased rollout look like - pilot team first?
questions_asked.answer: We typically recommend starting with one sales team (10-15 users) for 2 weeks, then expanding. Minimizes change management risk.

next_steps: Avery to brief Riley on technical fit
Schedule exec meeting to discuss business case

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Wyatt summarizing technical evaluation outcomes and confirming Avery's positive technical assessment. Shared proposed agenda for Riley White exec meeting: business case, ROI analysis, implementation timeline, and pricing discussion. Wyatt confirmed Riley's availability for two sessions next week.

questions_asked.question: Should we include Leo Mitchell and Blake Doyle in the exec meetings or separate procurement discussions?
questions_asked.answer: Let's start with Riley to get exec alignment, then bring in Leo and Blake for procurement and rollout planning.

next_steps: Exec meeting with Riley scheduled Tuesday & Thursday next week

touches.ai_summary: 37-minute exec meeting with Riley White (CRO). Presented business case: $62.3K annual investment vs 400+ hours/year time savings across management team, plus improved forecast accuracy. Riley focused on strategic value - competitive advantage in enterprise managed IT market. Discussed success metrics and executive dashboard requirements. Riley engaged and asking detailed questions about implementation.

questions_asked.question: What's your confidence level in the ROI projections?
questions_asked.answer: Conservative estimate based on 8hrs/week across 5 managers. Many customers see 12-15hrs/week savings once fully adopted. Forecast accuracy improvements harder to quantify but typically 15-20% reduction in variance.

questions_asked.question: How does this fit with our Q2 planning timeline?
questions_asked.answer: 3-week implementation means you'd have initial dashboards live before mid-May, full rollout by end of Q2 for Q3 planning cycle.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you personally in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Forecast accuracy within 10% variance, and my managers spending their time coaching reps instead of building spreadsheets. That's the operational excellence we need.

next_steps: Riley to review with finance
Second exec session to discuss terms and procurement process

touches.ai_summary: 32-minute second exec meeting with Riley White. Discussed procurement process - need to involve Leo Mitchell, Avery Wang, and Blake Doyle for vendor evaluation. Riley confirmed budget allocated and asked about contract terms, payment structure, and expansion options. Covered implementation timeline and success criteria. Riley wants proposal with detailed scope by end of week.

questions_asked.question: What's your preferred contract structure - annual or multi-year?
questions_asked.answer: We offer both. Multi-year provides 10-15% discount and locks in pricing. Annual gives more flexibility but higher per-seat cost.

questions_asked.question: If we expand beyond 51 seats next year, how does that work?
questions_asked.answer: True-up model - you add seats at prorated annual cost, then reconcile at renewal. No penalties for growth.

questions_asked.question: Who drives the procurement process on your end?
questions_asked.answer: Leo Mitchell will lead vendor evaluation and contracting. Blake and Avery will validate operational fit. Wyatt's my implementation lead.

next_steps: Send formal proposal by Friday
Schedule negotiation kick-off with Leo, Avery, Blake

touches.ai_summary: 26-minute exec meeting with Riley White focused on negotiation parameters. Riley flagged budget constraints and asked about discount options for 51-seat deal. Discussed implementation risk mitigation - phased rollout, training support, and success guarantees. Riley transparent about internal approval process: needs CFO sign-off for contracts >$50K.

questions_asked.question: What flexibility do you have on the $62.3K list price?
questions_asked.answer: We have some room for enterprise deals of this size. Typically 5-10% for annual commitments, more for multi-year.

questions_asked.question: What would make this an easy CFO approval?
questions_asked.answer: Clear ROI, reference customers in managed IT space, and minimized implementation risk. A phased payment structure could help too.

questions_asked.question: Is there a specific price point that makes this a no-brainer?
questions_asked.answer: If you can get to $55-56K range, that fits comfortably in my allocated budget without needing to justify overages.

objections: Need to stay within approved budget threshold - anything over $60K gets additional scrutiny from finance
next_steps: Prepare revised pricing proposal
Schedule pricing call with Wyatt

touches.ai_summary: 28-minute pricing call with Wyatt Yang. Discussed Riley's feedback on budget constraints. Wyatt confirmed $55-56K target and explained finance approval process. Reviewed discount scenarios: 10% for annual commitment brings it to $56.1K. Discussed payment terms - quarterly vs annual. Wyatt advocating internally for deal approval.

questions_asked.question: Would quarterly payments at $56K total work better than annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Quarterly would definitely help with cash flow. Let me validate with finance, but that could remove a barrier.

questions_asked.question: If we do quarterly payments, does that change the discount structure?
questions_asked.answer: Slight adjustment - quarterly typically means 8% vs 10% discount, so $57.3K total. Still within Riley's comfort zone.

questions_asked.question: What's your confidence level this gets approved at that number?
questions_asked.answer: High. Riley's already committed, we just need to make the numbers work for finance. $57K with quarterly payments should be smooth.

next_steps: Send updated proposal with quarterly payment terms
Wyatt to confirm finance approval timeline

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute negotiation call with Wyatt Yang. Reviewed updated proposal: $57.3K annual ($14.3K quarterly), 51 seats, automations_plus_consulting package. Discussed contract terms: 12-month initial term, auto-renewal, 30-day cancellation notice. Wyatt raised questions about implementation timeline guarantees and SLA terms from procurement team (Leo, Blake).

questions_asked.question: Can you commit to 3-week implementation in the contract?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can include implementation SLA with milestone dates. If we miss timeline, we credit 10% of Q2 payment.

questions_asked.question: What ongoing support is included vs additional cost?
questions_asked.answer: Consulting package includes quarterly strategy reviews, unlimited email support, and priority phone support. Onsite training would be additional.

questions_asked.question: When do you need final approval to meet Q2 timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally by end of next week. That gives us 4 weeks before end of Q2 to complete implementation.

objections: Procurement team (Leo) flagged that we need legal review of MSA terms - that could add 5-7 days to approval timeline
next_steps: Send MSA for legal review
Schedule final pricing alignment call

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute final pricing call with Wyatt Yang. Confirmed all terms: $57.3K annual, quarterly payments, 51 seats, 3-week implementation SLA. Wyatt reported legal review complete with minor redlines (liability caps, data processing terms). Discussed signature process and kickoff timeline. Wyatt confident in approval by end of week pending final Riley/CFO sign-off.

questions_asked.question: Any issues with the legal redlines?
questions_asked.answer: Standard enterprise terms - liability cap at 12-month fees, GDPR data processing addendum. All acceptable on our end.

questions_asked.question: Who signs first - Riley or CFO?
questions_asked.answer: Riley can authorize, CFO just needs notification for budgeting. We should have signature by Friday.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation assuming Friday signature?
questions_asked.answer: Week of May 5th. That puts us live by end of month, full rollout early June - perfect for Q3 planning.
